main.description: A trained or hand-written scoring rubric for Fluoddity organisms. The scoring code is a JS function body in the same shape the breeder's BYO scoring textarea accepts (returns a number from `v`, `fit`, `nov`, `align`, `verdict`, `gen`, `config`). Most rubrics are linear hyperplanes fit by hot-or-not on the canonical descriptor 4-vec, but the schema lets you publish anything that returns a number. Other surfaces can subscribe to a rubric as their active scoring lens — the playground, landing hero, torus, and arena will then sample from it instead of the default temperature fitness.

name.description: Human label for the rubric — e.g. 'reward structure, punish blowout' or 'alice's hot/not, 2026-05-29'.

description.description: Optional longer-form explanation of what this rubric rewards or how it was made.

schemaVersion.description: Rubric schema version, so future feature-space changes can migrate old rubrics.

scorer.description: JS function body. Variables in scope: v (descriptors: fill motion blowout struct), fit (default fitness), nov (novelty), align (momentum cos), verdict, gen, config. Must return a number.

scorerHash.description: First 12 hex chars of sha-256(scorer). Lets the forest group rubrics that are byte-identical across users, and lets expeditions cross-reference the rubric they used.

features.description: Names of the input features the scorer reads from `v` (or elsewhere). Lets viewers display a per-feature bar chart even without re-training.

weights.description: Optional JSON-stringified array of per-feature weights (for linear rubrics — same order as `features`).

bias.description: Optional JSON-stringified scalar bias for linear rubrics.

trainingSize.description: Number of judgments the rubric was fit on (0 for hand-written).

judgments.description: Optional JSON array of the raw judgments: [[label, fill, motion, struct, blowout], ...]. Lets others re-fit the rubric in a richer feature space or audit your training set.

accuracy.description: Optional JSON-stringified accuracy (0..1) on training data.

parent.description: Optional at-uri of a previous rubric this one was derived from (e.g. retrained on more judgments).

rubricForest.description: Constant link target (a DID) so Constellation can enumerate every rubric across users. Always did:web:g.mino.mobi — the same anchor the gallery and expeditions use, but under a different field path so the three collections enumerate independently.
